hypothesis.H3: Staleness correlates with DUE-item false alarm rate

H1.verdict: CANNOT TEST — only proxy-K has dual-observer (maintenance.py 3-tier logic). No other tool has fallback.
H1.detail: maintenance.py proxy-K has 3-tier: historical minimum → clean baseline → dirty post-compaction. This is the only true dual-observer. 11 other tools have single baselines.

H2.note: Revised threshold: >20 sessions is stale for this system (sessions are rapid). F-CON1 at 209s is ANCIENT — data lost.

H3.verdict: PARTIAL — staleness produces false signals but correlation requires DUE-item tracking per tool (not available)
H3.detail: dispatch_calibration R²=-0.089 means structural weights produce noise not signal. F-CON1 1.3% conflict rate baseline from S189 is irrelevant at current scale. Both produce wrong classifications but DUE-item attribution per tool not tracked.

prescriptions: Wire observer-age check into maintenance.py: flag tools with baselines >30 sessions stale
Deprecate F-CON1 baseline (S189, 209s stale) — replace or remove
Add staleness header to each tool ("baseline age: automatic" vs "baseline: S352, last refresh: S398")
Dual-observer pattern should be generalized: every tool baseline should have a fallback + staleness decay
